title: erigeron glabellus
intro: Erigeron glabellus, also known as pale inflorescenced fleabane, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer effects. Erigeron glabellus can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use, although further research is required to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
